page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is limited to a 33 GB temporary disk, but the instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. No GPU accelerators are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the D4s_v3 sits in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute. In stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, the instance scores in the bottom 25 percent, reflecting its older Intel Xeon v4 architecture. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with small-block cached reads landing in the bottom 10 percent and large-block uncached reads in the bottom 25 percent. However, the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating, placing it in the middle 50 percent of tested systems. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, BMI2, and FMA instructions, though AVX-512 is absent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4s_v3 offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and a standard memory-to-core ratio without the variable performance of burstable instances. However, its lower resource density and modest benchmark throughput present a tradeoff for compute-intensive tasks. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including light web serving, small database operations, development and testing environments, and basic application hosting where high-throughput processing is not a primary requirement.
description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 33 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage support. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory bandwidth performance mostly in the bottom 10 to 25 percent, though it maintains average PassMark memory scores. Lacking GPU acceleration and AVX-512 support, this instance represents a cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated resources without high-compute demands. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks such as light web serving, development environments, and small database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support, suitable for light web serving and development.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 33 GB local storage, ideal for basic development and testing.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine offering balanced memory-to-core ratios for entry-level workloads and development environments.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
33 GB local temporary storage with Premium Storage capability
Dedicated CPU allocation avoiding burstable performance degradation
